behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real Git rename-status contract plus CLI parsing/aggregation. A mocked Git response can hide real rename encodings (e.g., rename status formats, path quoting), so in-process tests are insufficient (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text contains no AI/ML step and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp Git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess against the repo, parse stdout report, and assert: rename count increments, add/delete counts do not include that same file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real Git/subprocess handling of binary diffs and real stdout/stderr/process-exit behavior. Stubbing diff text can miss decoding/process edge cases that only appear with actual binary files (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). No AI/ML dependence, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp Git repo, add a real binary file (e.g., random bytes), create changes including that file, execute the CLI subprocess, and assert successful exit plus correct binary-related count in stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD/no-commit behavior is a real Git repository state with specific command failures/messages. In-process mocks can bypass those exact external semantics, so real subprocess+repo state is needed (REAL-INFRA). Agentic/Workflow rejected: behavior text is deterministic Git/CLI handling only.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh Git repo with zero commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert non-error exit with an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Correctness is defined against actual repository history, which requires real Git history traversal and external command behavior. Mocked history responses can trivially align with expected counts and miss contract mismatches (MOCK-HIDE). No AI/ML output quality is involved; single deterministic pipeline, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a known sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies, run the CLI subprocess, and assert reported category counts equal the known ground truth from that real history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged is Git index/worktree state semantics, which are external and subprocess-driven. In-process fakes often miss real index behavior and flag combinations, so real repo + real CLI execution is required (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no model decision and no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run the CLI subprocess for each mode, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
